note: Keys must exactly match FG_SMARTS keys in constants/fg_smarts.py (plus 'Steroid' which uses Python detection). Rebuild db/fg_residue_table.csv after any key change.

Carboxylic acid.description: RC(=O)OH group. Strong H-bond donor + acceptor; ionisable (pKa ~4.5). Engages Asp/Glu/Arg/Lys in active sites via salt bridge or direct coordination.

Ester.description: Acyclic RC(=O)OR' group (ring esters are captured by 'Lactone'). H-bond acceptor only; metabolically labile. Hydrolysed by esterases and lipases; prodrug strategy.

Amide.description: RC(=O)NHR' group. Resonance-stabilised; H-bond donor + acceptor. Backbone motif of peptide substrates; preferred pharmacophore in protease/kinase inhibitors.

Lactone.description: Cyclic ester (-C(=O)-O- in ring). Includes gamma-lactones (5-membered), delta-lactones (6-membered), and aromatic lactones (coumarins). Superset of Coumarin and Macrolide. Hydrolysed by lactonases and esterases; HDAC inhibitors often contain a hydroxamic acid or lactone zinc-binding group; some nuclear receptor ligands are lactones.

Ketone.description: RC(=O)R' group (both neighbors are C). Pure H-bond acceptor. Targeted by oxidoreductases; electrophilic warhead in activated ketone inhibitors.

Aldehyde.description: RCH=O group. Reactive electrophile; forms Schiff bases with Lys. Substrate for ALDH/ADH; found in some natural product warheads.

Hydroxyl.description: Aliphatic -OH group (excludes phenols and acid/ester OH). H-bond donor + acceptor. Engaged by Ser/Thr/Tyr kinases; common in sugar and nucleotide ligands.

Phenol.description: ArOH group. Stronger H-bond donor than aliphatic OH (pKa ~10); can ionise. Key pharmacophore in oestrogen receptor ligands, COX inhibitors, and COMT substrates.

Catechol.description: Ortho-dihydroxybenzene motif. Strong metal chelator (Fe3+, Cu2+, Zn2+); COMT substrate. Present in quercetin, EGCG, catecholamines. Susceptible to oxidation.

Methylenedioxy.description: -OCH2O- bridge across two adjacent aromatic carbons (1,3-benzodioxole motif). Present in piperine, safrole, methylenedioxymethamphetamine. The methylene C is a site for CYP450 carbene intermediate formation; mechanism-based inhibitor of CYP450 and MAO.

Ether.description: C-O-C group (not carbonyl, not OH, not ester oxygen). Weak H-bond acceptor. Metabolised by CYP450 O-dealkylation; common in drug scaffolds for improving bioavailability.

Primary amine.description: RNH2 group (excludes amide, sulfonamide, aromatic N). Ionisable (pKa ~10); strong H-bond donor. Substrate for MAO and amine oxidases; engages Asp/Glu by salt bridge.

Secondary amine.description: R2NH group (excludes amide, sulfonamide, aromatic N). H-bond donor + acceptor. Common in GPCR ligands; substrate for MAO-B.

Tertiary amine.description: R3N group (excludes imines, amides, sulfonamides, aromatic N). Non-ionisable in physiological range; H-bond acceptor only. Found in muscarinic/nicotinic ligands.

Imidazole.description: Five-membered aromatic heterocycle with N1 and N3. Mimics histidine; coordinates metal ions (Fe, Zn, Cu). Key motif in CYP450 inhibitors (azole antifungals) and histamine receptor ligands.

Indole.description: Bicyclic aromatic scaffold: pyrrole ring fused to benzene. Defining pharmacophore of tryptamine alkaloids (serotonin, melatonin, psilocybin, vincristine). Indole NH is an H-bond donor; the pi-system engages Trp/Phe residues. Partially overlaps Phenyl ring (benzene portion still detected).

Nitrile.description: RC≡N group. Electrophilic carbon; forms reversible covalent adduct with Cys/Ser. Used as warhead in covalent protease inhibitors (e.g. nirmatrelvir).

Nitro.description: -NO2 group. Electron withdrawing; bioreduced to nitroso/hydroxylamine/amine by nitroreductases and CYP450. Found in some antibiotics (metronidazole) and kinase inhibitors.

Thiol.description: R-SH group. Strong nucleophile; coordinates metal ions (Zn2+, Fe, Cu). Undergoes disulfide exchange (Cys active sites). Key in cysteine proteases, thioredoxins, metalloenzymes.

Sulfonamide.description: RS(=O)2NR'2 group. Strong H-bond donor + acceptor. Inhibits carbonic anhydrase by direct zinc coordination via NH. Broad antimicrobial scaffold.

Phenyl ring.description: Unsubstituted or substituted benzene ring. Pi-stacking with Phe/Tyr/Trp; hydrophobic contacts. Ubiquitous in kinase inhibitors, GPCRs ligands, and nuclear receptor agonists/antagonists.

Coumarin.description: 2H-1-Benzopyran-2-one: benzene ring fused to a 6-membered aromatic lactone with the C=O at C2. Key scaffold in anticoagulants (warfarin), MAO inhibitors (umbelliferone), and CYP450-metabolised phytochemicals. Subset of Lactone; overlaps Phenyl ring (benzene portion).

Chromone.description: 4H-1-Benzopyran-4-one: benzene ring fused to a 6-membered ring with C=O at C4 and ring O at C1. Backbone of flavone, flavonol, and isoflavone scaffolds (quercetin, kaempferol, genistein). NOT a lactone (C=O is a ring ketone; ring O is an ether). Target class: COX, kinase, estrogen receptor, tyrosine kinase.

Steroid.description: Tetracyclic 6-6-6-5 fused ring scaffold (cyclopentanophenanthrene). A-B-C rings are six-membered, D ring is five-membered, all carbon. Detected via Python function _detect_steroid_core (SMARTS not used — see utils/fg_detector.py). Present in testosterone, cholesterol, cortisol, estradiol, bile acids.

Halogen.description: C-X bond (F, Cl, Br, I). F: metabolic block, lipophilicity tuning; Cl/Br: halogen bond donor to carbonyl O (backbone C=O of protein); I: bulky, used in X-ray structure work.

Epoxide.description: Three-membered ring with O. Highly reactive electrophile; alkylates Lys/Cys/His in active sites. Natural product warhead (terpenoids, mitomycin). Detoxified by epoxide hydrolase.

Endoperoxide.description: O-O bond within a ring (cyclic peroxide). Pharmacophore of artemisinin-class antimalarials; the endoperoxide bridge reacts with heme Fe(II) to generate carbon-centred radicals that alkylate parasite proteins. Distinct from acyclic organic peroxides and from the epoxide pattern.

α,β-unsat. carbonyl.description: Michael acceptor motif C=C-C=O. Undergoes 1,4-addition with soft nucleophiles (Cys-SH, Lys-NH2). Covalent warhead in natural products (curcumin, parthenolide, withaferin A) and designed covalent inhibitors.

Macrolide.description: Lactone in a macrocyclic ring of 12 or more atoms. Subset of Lactone; excludes all rings up to 11-membered. Pharmacophore class of erythromycin (14-membered, ribosome 50S), rapamycin/sirolimus (31-membered, mTOR complex), tacrolimus/FK506 (23-membered, calcineurin via FKBP12). Macrolides bind ribosomes (antibacterial), mTOR complex (immunosuppressant/anticancer), and calcineurin (immunosuppressant).
